parameters.description: PEM-encoded X.509 certificate bundle added to nodes' trust store; used when proxy is configured.
Policy for when to reference user-ca-bundle in Proxy trustedCA: Proxyonly when proxy configured, or Always. Optional.

citations.sectionHeading: 1.11.2. Configuring the cluster-wide proxy during installation

parameters.description: API version for install-config.yaml content. Current version is v1.
Base domain for cluster; used with metadata.name for routes. Full DNS name is <metadata.name>.<baseDomain>.
Controls installation of optional core cluster components.
Additional capabilities beyond baselineCapabilitySet.
Initial set of optional capabilities to enable.
Configuration for compute (worker) machines.
Instruction set architecture for the machine pool. All pools must match.
Enable or disable simultaneous multithreading on compute machines.
Name of the machine pool. Required if compute is used.
Cloud provider for worker machines on Nutanix IPI. Use nutanix (must match control plane platform).

parameters.allowed: non-negative integer; 0 for three-node compact when control plane replicas is 3
parameters.description: Worker machine count. Standard clusters use at least 3 workers; set 0 with control plane replicas 3 for a compact three-node cluster (OpenShift 4.20 Nutanix doc).
Configuration for control plane machines.

parameters.allowed: Array of Disk objects (etcd, swap, user-defined)
parameters.description: Disk partitioning and setup configuration for control plane machines. Configures dedicated disks for etcd data, swap space, or custom user-defined partitions. Applied via MachineConfigs during installation.
Platform-specific configuration for control plane machine pool.
Nutanix-specific configuration for control plane machines. Use for per-pool overrides. Overrides global platform.nutanix settings for control plane only.
Number of control plane nodes. Must be 3 or 5 for HA. SNO deployments use 1.
Instruction set architecture for control plane pool.
Enable or disable simultaneous multithreading on control plane machines.
Name of the machine pool. Required if controlPlane is used.
Cloud provider for control plane on Nutanix IPI. Use nutanix (must match compute platform).
Number of control plane machines for Nutanix IPI: 3 for standard or compact three-node (with compute.replicas 0), or 1 for single-node OpenShift (OpenShift 4.20 Nutanix install-config).
Enables workload partitioning to isolate cluster services to reserved CPUs. Only at install.
Cloud Credential Operator (CCO) mode. Installing on Nutanix §1.4 requires manual mode; this app always emits credentialsMode: Manual in install-config (no Mint/Passthrough for this platform).

citations.sectionHeading: 1.4. Configuring the Cloud Credential Operator utility

parameters.description: Custom feature gate configuration for enabling/disabling specific cluster features. Only valid when featureSet is 'CustomNoUpgrade'. Each entry is 'FeatureGateName=true' to enable or 'FeatureGateName=false' to disable. Example: ['ExperimentalFeature=true', 'OldFeature=false']. Clusters with custom feature gates cannot upgrade.
Feature set to enable (features not enabled by default).
Enable or disable FIPS mode. RHCOS uses FIPS-validated crypto when true.
One or more repositories that may contain the same images.
Repository users refer to (e.g. in image pull specs). Required if imageContentSources used.
Replacement for soon-to-be-deprecated imageContentSources; same sub-field structure (mirrors, source). Specifies the sources or repositories for the release-image content.

citations.docTitle: Installing a cluster on disconnected infrastructure

parameters.description: Same as imageContentSources[].mirrors. One or more mirror registry URLs that may contain the same images (e.g. registry.example.com:5000/ocp4/openshift4).

citations.docTitle: Installing a cluster on disconnected infrastructure

parameters.description: Same as imageContentSources[].source. Source repository for release-image content (e.g. quay.io/openshift-release-dev/ocp-release). Required if imageDigestSources used.

citations.docTitle: Installing a cluster on disconnected infrastructure

parameters.description: Kubernetes ObjectMeta; only name is consumed.

parameters.default: cluster name from Cluster Identity when not overridden
parameters.description: Cluster name (metadata.name). DNS records use <name>.<baseDomain>. Must match Cluster Identity in this app.
Configuration for the cluster network. Cannot be changed after installation.
IP address blocks for pods. Multiple blocks must not overlap.
IP address block for pods. IPv4 prefix 0-32, IPv6 0-128.
Subnet prefix length per node. hostPrefix 23 gives 510 pod IPs per node.
MTU for cluster network (pod-to-pod traffic). Defaults to 8951 for OVN-Kubernetes (Geneve overhead), 1450 for OpenShiftSDN.

citations.sectionHeading: OCP 4.20 installation configuration docs, networking section

parameters.description: IP address blocks for machines. Must match CIDR of preferred NIC.
IP network block for machines in CIDR notation.
Red Hat OpenShift Networking network plugin. Default is OVNKubernetes.

parameters.allowed: OVNKubernetesConfig object with ipv4, ipv6, policyAuditConfig fields
parameters.description: Configuration for OVN-Kubernetes CNI plugin (default network plugin for OpenShift 4.20). Only applies when networking.networkType is OVNKubernetes.
IPv4 join subnet used internally by ovn-kubernetes. Must not overlap node network. Cannot change after install.
IP address block for services. OVNKubernetes supports single block or dual-stack.
Configuration for the platform for installation.

parameters.allowed: nutanix.Platform object with prismCentral, prismElements, subnets, etc.
parameters.description: Nutanix platform configuration for installer-provisioned infrastructure. Includes Prism Central connection details, Prism Elements, subnets, storage containers. Required when platform.type is 'nutanix'.
API virtual IP address for single-stack IPv4 deployments. For dual-stack, use apiVIPs instead.

parameters.allowed: list of IPv4 and/or IPv6 addresses
parameters.description: API virtual IP addresses for dual-stack deployments. Single-stack: one element; dual-stack: two (IPv4 first, IPv6 second).
Optional Nutanix cluster (Prism Element) name for VM placement.

citations.docTitle: Installing a cluster on Nutanix with installer-provisioned infrastructure

parameters.description: Default machine pool configuration for Nutanix (cores, memory MB, disk size GB, boot type).

parameters.description: Default boot type for Nutanix machines.
Default Nutanix categories (key-value pairs) for all machines.
Nutanix failure domains for multi-cluster HA (name, prismElement, subnet UUIDs).

citations.sectionHeading: OCP 4.20 Nutanix docs, failure domain section

parameters.description: Ingress virtual IP address for single-stack IPv4 deployments. For dual-stack, use ingressVIPs instead.

parameters.allowed: list of IPv4 and/or IPv6 addresses
parameters.description: Ingress virtual IP addresses for dual-stack deployments. Single-stack: one element; dual-stack: two (IPv4 first, IPv6 second).
Nutanix Prism Central endpoint (endpoint, port) and credentials.

Prism Central FQDN or IP address (install-config platform.nutanix.prismCentral.endpoint.address).

parameters.description: Prism Central API port under prismCentral.endpoint (default 9440).
Prism Central password. Emitted in install-config only when including credentials.

parameters.description: Prism Central username. Emitted in install-config only when including credentials.

parameters.description: Cluster-wide proxy configuration for external HTTP/HTTPS connections. Configure if cluster nodes access Red Hat services, container registries, or external resources through a proxy server. If unset, cluster does not use a proxy.
Proxy URL for HTTP connections outside the cluster. URL scheme must be http.

parameters.description: Proxy URL for HTTPS connections outside the cluster.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of destinations to exclude from proxying. Prefix domain with . for subdomains only; use * to bypass proxy for all.

parameters.description: How to publish cluster endpoints. Internal is not supported on Nutanix IPI (validation error); the generator coerces Internal to External in install-config. Prefer External.
Pull secret from Red Hat OpenShift Cluster Manager to authenticate image pulls from Quay.io etc.
SSH key to authenticate access to cluster machines. Recommend for production.
